text: **Kelly:** Hi Anna.
**Kelly:** Why do you have all this {weather|天気/気象} stuff?
**Anna:** I love {weather|天気/気象}
**Kelly:** Me too!
**Kelly:** Weather is so {important|重要な}!
**Anna:** It is!
**Anna:** It {affects|影響を与える} people's lives
**Anna:** Have you ever {reported|報告した} on a big weather event?
**Anna:** I have.
**Anna:** I've {reported|報告した} on a {blizzard|吹雪/大雪}
**Kelly:** Do you mean the one last weekend?
**Anna:** Yes.
**Anna:** I had been {waiting|待っていた} for that {blizzard|吹雪/大雪} for years.
**Anna:** When it came, I was ready.
**Anna:** Welcome to our most {perfect|完璧な} lesson.
**Anna:** Why is it perfect?
**Anna:** Today we are {reviewing|復習する} the present perfect and past perfect verb tenses
**Anna:** These show that an action is {completed|完了した}
**Anna:** Kelly uses the present perfect when she says, Have you ever {reported|報告した} on a big weather event?
**Anna:** Anna uses the past perfect when she says.
**Anna:** I had been {waiting|待っていた} for that {blizzard|吹雪/大雪} for years
**Anna:** Listen for have or had and the past participle to find more sentences with the perfect tense.
**Anna:** I'll color those words to help you.
**Anna:** Kelly, I have {wanted|望んでいた} to report on a big weather event my whole life.
**Kelly:** Who hasn't?
**Kelly:** Did you report all weekend by yourself?
**Anna:** No, no, no, no.
**Anna:** I {volunteered|志願した} Pete to help me
**Pete:** Why am I here on a Saturday?
**Pete:** Why are you {carrying|運んでいる} things?
**Kelly:** Why?
**Anna:** Pete, these are my {supplies|物資/備品|/səˈplaɪz/|Emergency supplies are important.}
**Anna:** Food, a {blanket|毛布|/ˈblæŋkɪt/|A warm blanket keeps you cozy.}, warm clothing.
**Anna:** Where are your supplies?
**Anna:** Pete, Pete, Pete, Pete.
**Anna:** This could be the {blizzard|吹雪/大雪|/ˈblɪzərd/|A blizzard is very dangerous.} of the century.
**Pete:** It'll be fine.
**Kelly:** How else have you {prepared|準備した|/prəˈpɛrd/|Are you prepared for the trip?}?
**Anna:** Well, I had just bought the latest {weather|天気|/ˈwɛðər/|The weather is nice today.}
**Anna:** {forecasting|予報|/ˈfɔːrkæstɪŋ/|Weather forecasting helps plan activities.} software so I brought it.
**Kelly:** Do you mean the Weather Genie Pro?
**Anna:** You know it.
**Anna:** Pete thought it was pretty {great|素晴らしい|/greɪt/|That's a great idea!} too.
**Pete:** Do you have any games on that thing?
**Anna:** Yes, I have the best weather {survival|生存/survival|/sərˈvaɪvəl/|Survival skills are important.} game.
**Anna:** Boom!
**Pete:** Sounds fun.
**Anna:** It is, but right now, Pete, this computer is a work {tool|道具|/tuːl/|A hammer is a useful tool.}.
**Anna:** It will give us the {temperature|温度|/ˈtɛmprətʃər/|The temperature is very low today.}, wind speed, wind direction, and amount of snowfall
**Anna:** In real time.
**Anna:** Boom boom.
**Pete:** I can't wait.
**Anna:** Pete.
**Anna:** We need a name for this blizzard.
**Pete:** No, we don't.
**Anna:** All the great storms have names.
**Pete:** No, they don't.
**Anna:** I know.
**Anna:** The big snow.
**Anna:** I am not saying the big snow.
**Pete:** {Welcome|ようこそ/歓迎|/ˈwelkəm/|Welcome to our party.} to the {big|大きな} snow.
**Kelly:** The big snow {broke|壊した/記録を破った} all kinds of {records|記録}, didn't it?
**Anna:** Yes it did.
**Anna:** And every time a record was {broken|壊された/記録が破られた}, we {celebrated|祝った}.
**Anna:** So far in Washington, DC
**Anna:** 29 inches of snow has {fallen|降った}.
**Anna:** That, my dear {listeners|聴衆}, is a record!
**Anna:** We just broke the wind speed record!
**Anna:** Snow has been {falling|降っている} for 30 hours {straight|まっすぐ/連続}.
**Anna:** That's another record.
**Kelly:** By Saturday night, stores and {restaurants|レストラン} had closed.
**Kelly:** Did you bring enough
**Anna:** food.
**Anna:** I thought I had {brought|持ってきた} enough food, but I {ran out|なくなった}.
**Anna:** Hey Pete, where's my bag of {popcorn|ポップコーン}?
**Pete:** Maybe you ate it already?
**Anna:** No, I didn't
**Pete:** I haven't seen it.
**Anna:** We had {reported|報告した} together for 48 hours {straight|連続}.
**Kelly:** Wow.
**Anna:** That must have been a great {team building|チームビルディング} exercise for.
**Anna:** you and Pete.
**Anna:** Yeah, you could say that.
**Anna:** I hope you found all the sentences with perfect tenses.
**Anna:** Learn more on our website.
